description: Define embedded-image extraction scope to include HTML data URLs and HTML-referenced CID inline images
Parse inbound email HTML for data:image/*;base64 payloads and emit synthetic attachment records
Decode and validate extracted data URL images (base64 decode, mime detection, byte size)
Resolve cid: image references from HTML and map them to inline MIME parts provided in email attachment metadata
Skip unreferenced CID inline parts by default and process only HTML-referenced CID images
Generate deterministic synthetic attachment IDs and filenames for extracted embedded images
Run embedded-image extraction and persistence in new-ticket inbound email flow
Run embedded-image extraction and persistence in threaded-reply inbound email flow
Reuse storage-backed attachment ingestion path for embedded image synthetic attachments
Persist embedded images as external_files/documents and associate to tickets via document_associations
Apply idempotency for embedded image processing using deterministic synthetic attachment keys per message
Add a dedicated workflow action for original-source email persistence as .eml
Implement Gmail adapter retrieval of raw MIME/source message bytes for .eml persistence
Implement Microsoft adapter retrieval of raw MIME/source message bytes for .eml persistence
Implement MailHog/test path source MIME acquisition or deterministic fallback MIME generation
Persist one .eml file/document per inbound message and associate it to the target ticket
Apply idempotency for original .eml persistence using a reserved synthetic attachment key per message
Invoke original .eml persistence action once in new-ticket flow
Invoke original .eml persistence action once in threaded-reply flow
Keep embedded-image processing failures non-blocking for ticket/comment creation
Keep .eml persistence failures non-blocking for ticket/comment creation
Ensure system-user attribution is preserved for embedded-image and .eml created documents
Extend inbound email schemas/types to represent fields required for inline image and source MIME processing
Sanitize generated filenames and enforce image-only acceptance for embedded payload extraction
Update integration and workflow test fixtures to include embedded-image and source-email scenarios
IMAP webhook route performs only auth/validation + async handoff and returns quickly without byte-heavy processing in request path
IMAP ingress enforces hard caps for per-attachment bytes, total attachment bytes, attachment count, and raw MIME bytes before encoding/payload dispatch
IMAP webhook payload includes rawMimeBase64 and attachment byte fields (content/isInline/contentId/id/name/contentType/size) sufficient for document and .eml persistence
IMAP inbound processing path uses storage-backed/idempotent attachment persistence compatible with current documents schema (no legacy metadata-only insert path)
IMAP inbound path executes embedded extraction for HTML data URLs and referenced CID images before persistence
IMAP inbound path persists one original .eml document per inbound message from provided raw MIME bytes with deterministic filename/idempotency
Per-message attachment artifact processing in async path is sequential or strictly bounded to avoid Node event-loop starvation
Oversize/over-limit artifacts are skipped with structured reasons while ticket/comment creation remains successful
